text.title: Google Ads Campaign Setup Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Google Ads Campaign Setup MUST enforce. Rationale + source per rule. Rules referenced by id from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Conversion tracking MUST be verified firing in last 24 hours BEFORE creating any campaign. Skipping is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Smart Bidding without conversions falls back to broad match + Display + Partners; budget burns.

rule.statement: Campaign MUST be created with status=PAUSED. ENABLED on creation is forbidden.
rule.rationale: PAUSED gives time for ad-group + RSA + extension + verification; ENABLED-on-create triggers spend on broken setup.

rule.statement: Display Network + Search Partners MUST be explicitly disabled at campaign creation. API default differs from UI default.
rule.rationale: API defaults include Display + Partners; budget wastes if not explicitly disabled.

rule.statement: Every ad group MUST have >=1 RSA with 15 headlines + 4 descriptions. Single-RSA ad groups with <15 headlines are rejected.
rule.rationale: Google rewards 15-headline RSAs in Ad Strength + delivery; sub-15 ads cap reach.

rule.statement: Campaign MUST pass pre-launch checklist (tracking firing / RSA per group / extensions / conversion goal / negatives / budget) before ENABLED.
rule.rationale: Multi-item checks catch silent misconfigurations; no single check catches all.

rule.statement: If applicability preconditions are unmet (see AGENTS.md Skip If), the methodology MUST be skipped and a one-line skip-reason logged.
rule.rationale: A named skip terminal keeps decision-tree leaves resolvable and prevents silent misuse on misfit inputs.
